subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer 4 questions. You should indicate which 4 problems you wish to be graded. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the definitions of the terms Euclidean domain and principal ideal domain.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that a Euclidean domain must be a principal ideal domain.

problems.text: Prove that a finite integral domain must be a field.
Let \(R\) be a nonzero ring with 1 and \(I\) a proper ideal of \(R\). Prove that \(R\) has a maximal ideal that contains \(I\).
Let \(n \geq 2\) and let \(R\) be the ring of \(n \times n\) matrices with entries in a field \(F\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the only 2-sided ideals of \(R\) are the zero ideal and the whole ring.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a nonzero, proper left ideal of \(R\).

problems.text: Determine the conjugacy classes of elements of order 6 in \(\operatorname{GL}(4,\mathbb{Q})\), the group of invertible \(4 \times 4\) matrices with rational entries.

Let \(p\) be a prime and let \(\theta=e^{2\pi i/p}\in\mathbb{C}\), a primitive \(p\)-th root of unity. Prove that \(|\mathbb{Q}(\theta):\mathbb{Q}|=p-1\). (You may quote general criteria for irreducibility of polynomials, but irreducibility of particular polynomials must be explained.)
